div.head: 299. Telegram From the Department of State to the Embassy in PakistanSource: Department of State, Central Files, 890D.2321/3–1158. Secret. Drafted by Henry W. Spielman and approved by Rountree.
div.p:
2293. At Ankara Noon raised with Secretary possibility USG buying Pakistan’s exportable surplus cotton which he estimated 700,000 tons.The meeting took place on January 28. (Department of State, Secretary’s Memoranda of Conversation: Lot 64 D 199) You may inform Noon: (a)
Department has carefully looked into matter as Secretary promised; (b)
Information available to Department and Embassy, subject GOP correction, indicates no burdensome surplus Pak cotton at present time; (c)
Because its own cotton problems, USG regrets it not in position buy exportable surplus cotton from any country; (d)
In informal manner and as appropriate Department and Embassy officials will continue try to provide helpful advice to permit GOP retain and strengthen various aspects its trade ties with free world.
